highlights: AI Governance Specialist Company: The Boeing Company The Boeing Global Privacy Office (GPO), a subset of Boeing Law & Compliance, is seeking a Senior Global Privacy and AI Governance Specialist to support the operational development and execution of Boeing's end-to-end Global Privacy and AI Governance compliance lifecycle. This role is a senior position that reports to the Senior Leader, Global Privacy and AI Governance, and partners closely with Boeing's Responsible AI Program leaders and cross-functional teams, with a distinct focus on privacy-by-design, AI Risk Assessments, and lifecycle governance design, development, and execution. The ideal candidate brings deep, hands-on experience conducting global privacy assessments, operationalizing AI governance, and embedding compliance by design into complex, regulated environments. This person must exercise a high level of strategic judgment, autonomy, and accountability, while also collaborating effectively across a globally situated team to ensure that risk is identified, mitigated and minimized appropriately. This role is critical to translating strategy, regulatory requirements, and governance frameworks into repeatable, auditable, and scalable operational practices. This hybrid position can be based out of any of our major Boeing locations, including: Dallas, TX; Plano, TX; Arlington, VA; Seattle, WA; Auburn, WA; Everett, WA; Renton, WA; Mesa, AZ; or Seal Beach, CA; El Segundo, CA; Huntington Beach, CA; Hazelwood, MO; Berkeley, MO; Huntsville, AL; Englewood, CO; Titusville, FL; Chicago, IL; Oklahoma City, OK; Portland, OR; North Charleston, SC; or Ridley Park, PA. [...]
Data scientist role at a defense contractor focused on turning complex structured and unstructured data into actionable intelligence for government and private sector clients, with emphasis on machine learning, NLP, and AI solutions.
[...]
As a data scientist, you’re excited at the prospect of unlocking the secrets held by a data set, and you’re fascinated by the possibilities presented by IoT, machine learning, and artificial intelligence. In an increasingly connected world, massive amounts of structured and unstructured data open new opportunities. As a Data Scientist at Booz Allen, you can help turn these complex data sets into useful information to solve global challenges. Across private and public sectors—from fraud detection to cancer research to national intelligence—we need you to help find the answers in the data. 
On our team, you’ll use your analytical skills and data science knowledge to create real-world impact. You’ll work closely with your clients to understand their questions and needs, and then dig into their data-rich environments to find the pieces of their information puzzle. You’ll develop algorithms and systems and use the right combination of tools and frameworks to turn sets of disparate data points into objective answers to help clients make informed decisions. Ultimately, you’ll provide a deep understanding of the data, what it all means, and how it can be used.

* Experience developing predictive data models, quantitative analyses, or natural language processing

* Experience with visualization packages, including Plotly, Seaborn, or ggplot2
* Secret clearance
* Bachelor’s degree
